title: Foreign Currency Reserves 2026 – Market Notice 10 February 2026
summary: The Bank of England issued a US$2.5 billion 3-year eurobond on 10 February 2026 to finance its foreign currency reserves. The bond carries a 3.5% annual coupon and matures on 18 February 2029. This was the first operation under the Bank's updated issuance programme, which shifted to two benchmark issuances annually with greater transparency and a regular timetable.
key_points: Bank of England foreign currency reserves are managed separately from the Government's own foreign exchange reserves
In September 2025, BoE announced a shift to two benchmark issuances annually with regular timetable and high transparency
US$2.5 billion 3-year RegS/144A eurobond launched on 10 February 2026
Bond coupon is 3.5% per annum, maturing 18 February 2029
Joint Lead Managers were RBC Capital Markets, BMO Capital Markets, HSBC Bank plc, and Morgan Stanley
First 2026 operation in the Bank's updated issuance programme
why_it_matters: The Bank of England's shift to a structured, twice-yearly benchmark issuance programme for foreign currency reserves represents a change in sovereign debt issuance practices. This increases predictability for bond market participants and provides a regular supply of high-quality supranational/sovereign paper. The involvement of four major banks as joint lead managers (RBC, BMO, HSBC, Morgan Stanley) indicates standard institutional bond distribution via the RegS/144A structure targeting ECP/Professional investors.
macro_context: The Bank of England manages foreign currency reserves separately from the Government's own foreign exchange reserves, holding them in support of policy objectives. The issuance timing and size reflect reserve management needs rather than monetary policy signals.
market_structure_context: The move to two annual benchmark issuances with a designated group of banks (joint lead managers) formalises the issuance process and creates a more structured market for BoE foreign currency reserve bonds. The RegS/144A structure indicates institutional-only distribution targeting ECP/Professional investors under FCA/ICMA stabilisation provisions.
notes: The source is a legitimate Bank of England market notice with substantive content about a bond issuance. The notice contains significant legal disclaimers and forward-looking statement language common to securities announcements. Note: the published_at metadata (2026-03-27) may reflect the article listing date rather than the original issuance date stated in the notice body (10 February 2026). The 3.75% mentioned in related news about Bank Rate appears in the sidebar and is not directly relevant to this specific issuance.
